# generated by Fast Light User Interface Designer (fluid) version 1.0304
ti_lpc_app
Sel
select a vsm rom, need to do this for both roms as they are paired
Play
play current lpc data
Stop
stop playing lpc data
rom0:
enter a rom filename for addr: 0x0000 (16KB), remember roms are paired, so select both together
gain
adj audio level to sound hardware
 
lpc addresses from rom, right click on an address to render audio
LPC hex byte strings
lpc hex byte stream, one stream per line, right click on a line to render audio
Speak Spell's Rom Contents
word lists read from both roms( only valid for speak & spell roms pairs), right click on an address on the left to render audio
tms5xxx code tables
enter hex byte (or decimal number) strings, one line per param, chirp wfm, energy, pitch count, pitch, 10 lattice iir filter(k0-->k9) lookup codes, click on buttons at right to see some examples (chirp=10,-5,... for decimal, chirp_hx=0A,0xa,... for hex)
addr:
enter a rom address to utter (hex)
AEdit
open audio editor, edit script/bat to start your favourite audio editor, see:  Linux: 'open_audio_editor.sh',   Win: 'open_audio_editor.bat'
interp
select to enable interpolation of lpc parameters within each frame
filter
enable lattice iir filtering - the articulatory path beyond the vocal chords
pc srate:
set this to match the current samplerate of pc's audio hardware, if incorrectly set you will get pitch and duration errors (chipmunk/slomo)
+
go up one address and play
-
go down one address and play
+4
go up 4 addresses and play
-4
go down 4 addresses and play
rom1:
enter a 2nd rom filename for addr: 0x4000 (16KB), remember roms are paired, so select both together
Sel
select a vsm rom, need to do this for both roms as they are paired
lpc hex:
lpc hex byte stream (from last rendering), click play on right to re-render audio
Play
play lpc hex data
pitch6bits
unticked is 5bits 'pitch_count=32', ticked is 6bits 'pitch_count=64', see the tms5xxxx code table editbox
au srate:
sample rate to use when saving the .au audio file
au file gain
set the audio gain when saving to audio file, 100 is unity
au fname:
enter .au filename to save audio into
tms5100
select TMS5xxx chip version and its specific codes for: chirp, engery, pitch and lattice iir filter coeffs
tms5220
select TMS5xxx chip version and its specific codes for: chirp, engery, pitch and lattice iir filter coeffs
+last
add last rendered byte count to address and play
tms5200
select TMS5xxx chip version and its specific codes for: chirp, engery, pitch and lattice iir filter coeffs
lpc decml:
lpc decimal number stream (from last rendering), click play on right to re-render audio
Play
play lpc decimal data
B.File
play lpc hex data from a binary file
Play
play lpc at address
Enter
enter address in list
delay:
delay in secs between auto address stepping, e.g: 0.5
delta:
amount to auto address step by, e.g: 2     or    -1
Start
start auto address stepping
Stop
stop auto address stepping
count:
count of the number of auto address steps attempted
snd end:
end address reached in rom after speech was sounded (in hex), will show number of bytes parsed if sounding was not done using a rom address (hex)
-
dec address by defined amount
enter a value to dec/inc address by (decimal)
+
inc address by defined amount
